basic_details.job_title: DTU Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Offline for 108 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Delhi Technological University (Formerly Delhi College of Engineering)
basic_details.short_information: This section gives a fast overview of the DTU Apprenticeship Bharti 2026 so candidates can check the key facts at a glance before going through the detailed trade-wise breakdown below.

qualification.details: Eligibility for DTU Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is qualification-based rather than age-based, since the notification prescribes a specific diploma or degree for each trade instead of a common minimum qualification. Candidates need to check the required educational qualification against their applied trade before submitting the form. | Diploma Apprentices need a relevant Diploma such as Electrical, Mechanical, Civil/Environmental, ECE, or IT/CS Engineering, while Graduate Apprentices need a relevant Bachelor's degree such as B.Tech, BCA, B.Sc., or Bachelor in Library Science depending on the trade applied for. Candidates applying for posts like Applied Mathematics or Applied Physics need a Graduate/Diploma with computer knowledge or an MSc/B.Sc. (PCM) respectively, as listed in the trade-wise vacancy table above. | All candidates applying for DTU Apprenticeship 2026-27 must first register themselves on the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme portal at nats.education.gov.in, since applications without NATS registration will not be accepted. This registration is a mandatory prerequisite and must be completed before submitting the physical application form to DTU.

salary_details.details: Selected apprentices under DTU Apprenticeship Recruitment 2026 receive a fixed monthly stipend rather than a regular pay scale, since apprenticeship is a training engagement and not a permanent job. The stipend amount depends on the qualification level of the apprentice for the given trade. | Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

selection_process: Selection for DTU Apprenticeship Trainees 2026-27 is based on screening of applications followed by a document verification interview
rather than a written examination. Shortlisted candidates are required to appear in person with original documents for final verification before engagement.

NATS Portal Registration – Candidates register on the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme portal before applying. Application Submission – The filled application form with self-attested documents is sent by post to DTU before 31 July 2026. Screening of Applications – DTU shortlists eligible candidates based on the qualification prescribed for each trade. Document Verification/Interview – Shortlisted candidates carry original and photocopies of all relevant documents for verification at the time of interview. Engagement as Apprentice – Selected candidates are engaged as Apprentice Trainees for a period of one year.
how_to_apply: Although the final application form for DTU Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is submitted offline by post, the process begins online through mandatory NATS portal registration. Here is the complete step-by-step process to apply.
Visit the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme portal at nats.education.gov.in and complete candidate registration.
Download the application form from the DTU official website www.dtu.ac.in or use the format provided in the advertisement.
Fill the application form in block letters, mentioning Advertisement No., NATS Enrolment No., trade applied for, and complete educational qualification details.
Affix a passport-size photograph in the space provided on the form.
Attach self-attested copies of the 10th certificate, 12th certificate, Diploma certificate (if applicable), B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc. certificate, and identity proof.
Superscribe the envelope as "Application for engagement of Apprentice Trainee (2026-27) applied for………" mentioning the trade name.
Send the complete application by post to: The Assistant Registrar (Gen. Admin), Delhi Technological University, Shahbad Daulatpur, Bawana Road, Delhi-110042.
Ensure the application reaches DTU on or before the last date, 31 July 2026.

content_sections.heading: DTU Apprenticeship Notification PDF Download Link
content_sections.text: Candidates who want to verify the complete list of trades, qualifications, and stipend details before applying should download the official DTU Apprenticeship Trainees 2026-27 notification PDF along with the prescribed application form. Both documents are available on the official Delhi Technological University website under the recruitment/careers section at www.dtu.ac.in . Download DTU Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF Here DTU Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Application Form Here

content_sections.heading: DTU Apprentice Recruitment 2026: Quick Overview
content_sections.text: This section gives a fast overview of the DTU Apprenticeship Bharti 2026 so candidates can check the key facts at a glance before going through the detailed trade-wise breakdown below.

rows.dtu_apprenticeship_trainees_2026_27: Delhi Technological University (Formerly Delhi College of Engineering)

content_sections.heading: DTU Apprenticeship Important Dates 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates applying for DTU Apprentice Recruitment 2026 should note that this is a rolling application process with a single fixed submission deadline rather than a multi-stage exam calendar. The table below lists the only official date mentioned in the notification.

content_sections.heading: DTU Apprentice Vacancy 2026: Trade-wise Breakdown
content_sections.text: The DTU Apprenticeship Vacancy 2026 covers 22 trade categories spread across engineering, IT, library, and administrative departments, with Mechanical (Lab) carrying the highest number of seats at 25. The table below lists every trade along with its required qualification, vacancy count, and monthly stipend.

content_sections.heading: DTU Apprenticeship Eligibility Criteria 2026
content_sections.text: Eligibility for DTU Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is qualification-based rather than age-based, since the notification prescribes a specific diploma or degree for each trade instead of a common minimum qualification. Candidates need to check the required educational qualification against their applied trade before submitting the form. Diploma Apprentices need a relevant Diploma such as Electrical, Mechanical, Civil/Environmental, ECE, or IT/CS Engineering, while Graduate Apprentices need a relevant Bachelor's degree such as B.Tech, BCA, B.Sc., or Bachelor in Library Science depending on the trade applied for. Candidates applying for posts like Applied Mathematics or Applied Physics need a Graduate/Diploma with computer knowledge or an MSc/B.Sc. (PCM) respectively, as listed in the trade-wise vacancy table above. All candidates applying for DTU Apprenticeship 2026-27 must first register themselves on the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme portal at nats.education.gov.in , since applications without NATS registration will not be accepted. This registration is a mandatory prerequisite and must be completed before submitting the physical application form to DTU.

content_sections.heading: DTU Apprentice Recruitment 2026: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection for DTU Apprenticeship Trainees 2026-27 is based on screening of applications followed by a document verification interview, rather than a written examination. Shortlisted candidates are required to appear in person with original documents for final verification before engagement. NATS Portal Registration – Candidates register on the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme portal before applying. Application Submission – The filled application form with self-attested documents is sent by post to DTU before 31 July 2026. Screening of Applications – DTU shortlists eligible candidates based on the qualification prescribed for each trade. Document Verification/Interview – Shortlisted candidates carry original and photocopies of all relevant documents for verification at the time of interview. Engagement as Apprentice – Selected candidates are engaged as Apprentice Trainees for a period of one year.

content_sections.heading: DTU Apprenticeship Apply Online 2026 (NATS Registration & Application Process)
content_sections.text: Although the final application form for DTU Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is submitted offline by post, the process begins online through mandatory NATS portal registration. Here is the complete step-by-step process to apply.

content_sections.heading: DTU Apprentice Stipend 2026 (Salary Details)
content_sections.text: Selected apprentices under DTU Apprenticeship Recruitment 2026 receive a fixed monthly stipend rather than a regular pay scale, since apprenticeship is a training engagement and not a permanent job. The stipend amount depends on the qualification level of the apprentice for the given trade. Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

content_sections.heading: DTU Apprenticeship Document Requirements
content_sections.text: Candidates applying for DTU Apprentice Vacancy 2026 must enclose self-attested copies of specific certificates along with the application form, and carry the originals at the time of interview for verification. Missing documents at either stage can lead to rejection of candidature. 10th Certificate & Mark Sheet 12th Certificate & Mark Sheet Diploma Certificate & Mark Sheet (where applicable) B.Tech/B.Sc./B.E. Certificate & Mark Sheet (where applicable) Identity Proof Passport-size photograph affixed on the application form NATS Enrolment proof

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid While Applying for DTU Apprentice Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Many applicants lose their chance at DTU Apprenticeship 2026-27 due to small but avoidable errors in the application process. Being aware of these common mistakes in advance can help ensure a smooth submission. Skipping NATS registration: Applications without prior registration on the NATS portal will not be accepted by DTU. Not superscribing the envelope correctly: The envelope must clearly mention "Application for engagement of Apprentice Trainee (2026-27) applied for" along with the trade name, or it may be misplaced during scrutiny. Handwritten applications: The form must be typed by the candidate as specified in the notification, not handwritten. Missing self-attested copies: All educational certificates and mark sheets must be self-attested and enclosed; unattested or missing copies can lead to rejection. Sending application after the deadline: Since submission is by post, candidates should mail the form well before 31 July 2026 to ensure it reaches DTU on time. Choosing the wrong trade/qualification match: Candidates must apply only for trades matching their actual qualification, as listed in the vacancy table.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for DTU Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for the application to reach DTU is 31 July 2026, and candidates must complete NATS registration before applying.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in DTU Apprenticeship 2026-27?
faq.answer: There are 108 total vacancies for Graduate and Diploma Apprentice Trainees across 22 trades including Electrical, Mechanical, IT, ECE, CSE, and Library.

faq.question: Q3. What is the mode of application for DTU Apprentice Bharti 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must first register on the NATS portal online, then submit the typed application form by post to the Assistant Registrar (Gen. Admin), DTU, on or before 31 July 2026.

faq.question: Q4. What is the stipend for DTU Apprentice Trainees?
faq.answer: Diploma Apprentices receive a monthly stipend of ₹10,900, while Graduate Apprentices receive ₹12,300 per month, depending on the trade.

faq.question: Q5. Is NATS registration mandatory for DTU Apprenticeship Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, registration on the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme portal (nats.education.gov.in) is mandatory, and applications without it will not be accepted.

faq.question: Q6. What documents are required for DTU Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates need self-attested copies of 10th, 12th, Diploma, and B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc. certificates and mark sheets, along with identity proof and a passport-size photograph.

faq.question: Q7. What is the duration of the DTU apprenticeship?
faq.answer: The apprenticeship engagement is for a period of one year, as stated in the official DTU notification.

faq.question: Q8. Does DTU offer a job after completion of the apprenticeship?
faq.answer: No, DTU has clarified that upon completion of the apprenticeship, the university has no obligation to offer employment to any apprentice under any circumstances.

faq.question: Q9. Which trade has the highest vacancy in DTU Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Mechanical (Lab requirement) has the highest vacancy with 25 posts, followed by Electrical with 17 posts.

faq.question: Q10. Where can candidates check the official DTU Apprenticeship notification and apply online?
faq.answer: The official notification, application form, and NATS registration link are available on the Delhi Technological University website at www.dtu.ac.in.
